'''Naat Reath''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Naat Reath and her brother, [[Stam Reath|Stam]], were given to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by their parents. Studying together at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] which would plague the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] for nearly a decade, Reath remained at the Temple when her brother and his tutor, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Echuu Shen-Jon]], were called to [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|fight]] on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] for the opening salvo of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. During the fighting, Stam Reath confronted the [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] [[Sev'rance Tann/Legends|Sev'rance Tann]] and was slain leaving Shen-Jon and Reath in great emotional pain.<ref name="GGCC" />

[[File:Naat_Reath_captured.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Naat Reath captured during the [[Battle of Krant]].]]

In an attempt to help the pair grieve, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] assigned Reath to Shen-Jon as his new Padawan. Taking up the rank of [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Commander]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Reath was placed in command of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] who would follow her orders in battle. Eager to prove herself a worthy apprentice, Reath also longed to avenge her brother despite the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code's]] negative view on vengeance. Hunting [[General/Legends|General]] Taan across the galaxy, the pair hoped to rob the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] of one of their most aspiring generals, but their actions brought them dangerously close to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. When they finally tracked Tann to the planet [[Krant]], Reath was captured by the Acolyte and held prisoner. Challenging Shen-Jon to a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] for the prize of keeping Reath alive, Shen-Jon fell to the dark side and was able to defeat Taan in combat. When he set out to mutilate the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] woman's body, Reath stopped her master, begging him to abandon his hate and return to Coruscant with her. Upon his refusal, Reath returned to the Temple and reported to the High Council that Shen-Jon had killed Tann but died from wounds suffered in battle. However, Reath's testimony to the High Council was met with mixed reception. Mace Windu in particular did not fully believe Reath, and wondered for the rest of his life what happened on Krant. Windu asked Reath about the subject on at least one other occasion, but she spoke evasively, leaving the truth unclear to Windu and the High Council.
Reath's ultimate fate into the Rise of the Empire remained unknown, but when her Master Echuu Shen-Jon resurfaced decades later, she did not.<ref name="GGCC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Naat Reath's appearance in the briefing screen is taken from a promotion picture of [[Immi Danoo]].
